summary: Every artifact has its versioning model declared and traceable — versioning is not inferred, it is enforced at the boundary.

rules.rule: Every tool/script declares its SemVer-lite (MAJOR.MINOR) version in a VERSION constant or manifest, bumping MAJOR on breaking changes to CLI args, return shape, exit codes, or documented behavior contract.

A declared app/package versioning model points at a live version source (a derived version, not a hand-typed literal that can freeze), with the deployed version + commit hash exposed at the deploy boundary and health endpoint.

Every task/PR is classified before implementation with exactly one canonical VERSIONING: verdict read from the repo's local versioning authority — and if that authority is missing or contradictory the verdict is BLOCKED-CONFLICT, which stops work instead of inferring a bump.
Never ship a breaking change without the explicit declaration — a feat(scope)!: marker or BREAKING CHANGE: footer is required; a breaking change without it is non-compliant.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never use floating versions (^, ~, >=) for internal monorepo dependencies — exact pins only.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never edit an ADR body after ACCEPTED — only status transitions are allowed; a revised decision is captured in a new superseding ADR.
